selection.selection_note: All 26 repaired rows from owned_repair_batch17.json were judged against semantic_brief plus S-101/OpenCPN/Aqua Map/provider witnesses where present.

verdicts.expected: BUAARE02 is built-up area. It is used as a built-up area. Required geometry: symbol silhouette must match the strongest S-101/OpenCPN/Aqua Map reference. Required colours: reference-defined. Required geometry: symbol silhouette must match the strongest S-101/OpenCPN/Aqua Map reference.
verdicts.judge_comments: Candidate restores the built-up-area point cue as one brown filled dot and removes the prior cluster/baseline. This is not final human approval.
verdicts.observed: Candidate is a single compact brown built-up-area dot; the added black outline is a Helm styling accent, but the symbol class and filled-dot cue match the OpenCPN/S-101 witnesses.
verdicts.required_change: No repair required for this visual rerun; keep pending final approval QA and do not mark final-approved.

verdicts.expected: BUIREL01 is non-conspicuous religious building, Christian. It is used as a non-conspicuous religious building, Christian. Required geometry: symbol silhouette must match the strongest S-101/OpenCPN/Aqua Map reference.

verdicts.observed: Candidate draws a church-like outline with a Latin-cross top and baseline, while the OpenCPN/S-101 witnesses use the compact Christian religious-building cross/propeller-like mark.
verdicts.required_change: Redraw as the compact brown Christian religious-building witness shape from S-101/OpenCPN; remove the church-outline body and baseline.

verdicts.expected: BUIREL05 is mosque or minaret. It is used as a mosque or minaret. Required geometry: symbol silhouette must match the strongest S-101/OpenCPN/Aqua Map reference. verdicts.observed: Candidate uses a side-facing crescent beside the stem and only a bottom dot; the references show the mosque/minaret crescent spanning over the stem with a circular base/dot cue.
verdicts.required_change: Redraw with the crescent over the stem and the circular base/dot cue from the S-101/OpenCPN mosque/minaret witness; do not use a side moon glyph.

verdicts.expected: CHIMNY01 is chimney. It is used as a chimney. Required geometry: symbol silhouette must match the strongest S-101/OpenCPN/Aqua Map reference. Required colours: reference-defined. Required geometry: symbol silhouette must match the strongest S-101/OpenCPN/Aqua Map reference.
verdicts.judge_comments: Candidate does not meet the visual rerun gate; repair remains required before any final approval path.
verdicts.observed: Candidate restores a chimney stack and smoke cap but omits the base ring/dot seen in S-101/OpenCPN/Aqua Map and simplifies the smoke/stack structure too far.
verdicts.required_change: Redraw the brown chimney with vertical stack, top smoke form, and the base ring/dot marker; keep the chimney class instead of a generic tower.

verdicts.expected: CHIMNY11 is conspicuous chimney. It is used as a conspicuous chimney. Required geometry: symbol silhouette must match the strongest S-101/OpenCPN/Aqua Map reference. Required colours: black. Required geometry: symbol silhouette must match the strongest S-101/OpenCPN/Aqua Map reference. Required colours: black.

verdicts.required_change: Redraw the black conspicuous chimney with vertical stack, top smoke form, and the base ring/dot marker.

verdicts.expected: CURSRB01 is cursor with open centre. It is used as a cursor with open centre. Required geometry: symbol silhouette must match the strongest S-101/OpenCPN/Aqua Map reference. Required colours: reference-defined. Required geometry: symbol silhouette must match the strongest S-101/OpenCPN/Aqua Map reference.
verdicts.judge_comments: Candidate does not meet the visual rerun gate; repair remains required before any final approval path.
verdicts.observed: Candidate has four separated orange cursor arms, but it still places a center dot where the semantic brief and references require an open centre.
verdicts.required_change: Remove the center dot and leave a clean open gap between the four orange cursor arms.
